substep.title: Load Kipling Reference
substep.action: Load references/kipling-implementation-questions.md into working memory. Identify the per-tag emphasis order for {{primary_tag}}.

substep.title: Inventory the Known
substep.action:
Terse, one-line-per-fact summary of what is currently established from: - Ticket body (scope, AC, decisions inline) - Linked DRs (immutable decisions) - Prior turn findings in {{research_path}} (if any) - CONTEXT.md glossary (project vocabulary already resolved)
The point: identify what does NOT need re-investigation.

substep.title: Inventory the Unknown
substep.action:
Enumerate gaps in code-layer categories per the per-tag emphasis order. For each gap, name the Kipling category and the specific question shape (per the reference's "Typical question shapes" column).
Be honest. Suppressed unknowns become surprises during D-phase or implementation.

substep.title: Apply Grill-with-Docs Discipline
substep.action:
Cross-reference the ticket's vocabulary against CONTEXT.md. Note any: - Term conflict (ticket says X, CONTEXT.md says Y for same concept) - Fuzzy term (ticket uses an overloaded word; needs precision) - Scenario-bound ambiguity (concrete edge case not covered)
Note these for step 3 (user-question channel) and step 5 (CONTEXT.md inline update).

substep.title: Pick This Turn's Focus
substep.action:
Select ONE highest-value gap to close this turn. Heuristics: - Closest to scope > deeper detail - Blocks other gaps > stands alone - Single-question shape > multi-pronged - User-answerable now > research-bound
Store as {{turn_focus}}: one-sentence description.
Cap the turn's question count at 4 (matches the 4-subagent dispatch cap).
